title: What Happens When the Tradwife Dream Goes Wrong?
description: The hit novel Yesteryear seems to be a withering critique of influencers—but is actually more attuned to the corruptions of power.
hook: A novel about tradwife influencers that looks like critique but actually understands how power corrupts from the inside.

title: Reckoning With the Desires of China’s One-Child Generation
description:
I first encountered M Lin’s writing during our second year of graduate school in the MFA program at Brooklyn College. Her stories were, on my first reading, luminous and unpretentious, chronicling the often conflicting sexual, emotional, and political desires of women from China’s millennial One-Child Generation. Her debut collection, The Memory Museum, is a nostalgic […]

The post
hook: Chinese millennial women's tangled desires under the one-child policy—luminous stories about what a generation inherited and rejected.

title: His Girlfriend’s Love Is as Poisonous as a Mushroom
description:
“Wild Food” by Jess Gibson Sebastian saw Emily’s internet search history on the afternoon before the dinner party. When he’d checked his email on the desktop in their home study, the browser had been open. He always closed it himself, which cleared the cache. Total transparency in relationships was overrated, he felt. But Emily had […]

The post His Girlfriend’s Love Is as Poisonous as a Mushroom ap
hook: A dinner party, a browser history, and poisonous mushrooms—a story about surveillance, intimacy, and what we hide from lovers.

title: Barack Obama in the Trump Era
description: The reporter Peter Slevin asks the former President the question on many Democrats’ minds: Why isn’t he doing more in a time of crisis?
hook: Peter Slevin corners Obama with the question haunting Democrats: Why fade to the background when the country is burning?

title: The climate crisis is coming for your groceries
description: This story was originally published by Grist and is reproduced here as part of the Climate Desk collaboration. Two years ago, an intense heat wave engulfed much of Brazil. For five days at the end of April 2024, temperatures in the central and southern regions climbed to sweltering heights. Many affected were still reeling from another extreme heat wave […]
hook: Brazil's heat waves are destroying coffee and chocolate crops—your favorite foods are becoming casualties of climate collapse.
